codeQualityIssues.description: PipelineOrchestrator.execute() logs the runId in error message via string interpolation: 'Pipeline execution failed for run ${run.id}'. While not a security issue here (UUID), using structured logging with MDC (already set) would be more consistent.

testCoverage: 22 tests: 8 PipelineOrchestratorTest (full execution, MDC set, MDC cleared on failure, failed step recording, resume skip, step ID reuse, auto-determine start step), 5 PipelineOrchestratorIT (end-to-end with MockLlmAdapter + WireMock, step persistence, failure recording, resume from failure, MDC), 9 PipelineServiceTest (trigger + async, idempotency duplicate, force bypass, resume FAILED, resume from step, not found, not resumable COMPLETED, not resumable RUNNING, PARTIALLY_FAILED resumable). All 6 VAL-ORCH assertions covered.
summary: Well-structured orchestrator with clean step-level persistence and resume-from-failure support. executeOrLoadStep uses reified generics for type-safe deserialization. PipelineService correctly handles idempotency and resume validation. @Async dispatch properly configured after bean name collision fix. MDC context management in try/finally block.
